name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description: Ghostwriter Maven Plugin is an advanced documentation automation tool for Java projects. It automatically scans, analyzes, and assembles project documentation using embedded guidance tags and AI-powered synthesis. GW Maven Plugin 1.2.2-SNAPSHOT API documents a Maven plugin that integrates Machai Ghostwriter into Maven builds so guided, AI-assisted updates can be performed directly from standard Maven goals. The overview documentation states that the plugin is designed for repositories that keep instructions close to maintained files through embedded @guidance: blocks, scans configured directories, gathers guidance instructions, resolves project context, and delegates file processing to the Ghostwriter engine. Documented primary responsibilities include exposing Maven goals for guided file processing and interactive act execution, scanning project content for embedded @guidance: instructions, passing shared configuration such as model selection, excludes, instructions, and credentials to the processing engine, integrating with Maven session, project, reactor, and settings metadata, providing class-discovery helper tools so Ghostwriter workflows can inspect project classes and dependencies, and cleaning temporary processing artifacts generated during guided workflows. The package org.machanism.machai.gw.maven contains the core mojos and shared infrastructure, including AbstractGWMojo as the base class for GWMojo Maven plugin goals, GWMojo as the aggregator guided processing goal, GWPerModuleMojo as the per-module reactor-oriented guided processing goal, ActMojo as the interactive predefined action goal, ActPerModuleMojo as the per-module action variant, and CleanMojo as the temporary-artifact cleanup goal. The package org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools contains helper types for Java class discovery and reflection metadata, centered on scanning Maven project compile and output classpaths, locating classes by simple-name pattern, and returning structured metadata such as modifiers, members, annotations, class origin paths, source file locations, and dependency artifact coordinates. The generated Javadoc describes support for both aggregator-style execution and per-module reactor execution, making the plugin suitable for single-module documentation projects, multi-module Maven builds, and repository-wide content maintenance workflows. Effective build metadata identifies packaging type maven-plugin, project URL https://machai.machanism.org/gw-maven-plugin, Java release 8, and direct dependencies on org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter, org.apache.commons:commons-lang3, com.google.guava:guava, org.apache.maven.shared:maven-shared-utils, org.apache.maven:maven-core, org.apache.maven.plugin-tools:maven-plugin-annotations, org.codehaus.plexus:plexus-interactivity-api, and org.slf4j:slf4j-api. This artifact is best understood as a Maven build plugin for guided documentation processing, prompt-driven action execution, Maven reactor-aware workflows, GenAI credential forwarding from settings.xml, and controlled project classpath introspection during AI-assisted documentation maintenance.

usageContext: Running guided file processing from Maven goals over files containing embedded @guidance: blocks
Applying prompt-driven documentation actions from standard Maven command lines
Processing repositories in aggregator mode or per-module Maven reactor mode
Loading provider credentials from Maven settings.xml through a configured server identifier
Registering project-aware class discovery helpers for AI-assisted workflow execution
Maintaining documentation trees such as src/site inside Java and Maven projects

constructors.description: Creates the Maven goal implementation for gw:gw. Javadoc describes this class as the Maven goal that runs Ghostwriter guided file processing for a project. It is documented as an aggregator goal that can be executed even when a pom.xml is not present in the current directory and processes modules in reverse order, with sub-modules handled before parent modules.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, install the ready-to-use Maven plugin by declaring org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT in the build plugins section of pom.xml. Step 2, keep documentation or other maintained files under a scan root such as src/site and add embedded @guidance: blocks where guided processing should occur. Step 3, invoke the gw goal so Maven creates org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo during execution.

How to use it: Step 1, configure provider credentials in ~/.m2/settings.xml under the server id CodeMie if the selected model requires authentication. Step 2, run the guided processing goal from the project root. Step 3, Maven instantiates org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo, resolves shared configuration, scans the selected files, and delegates guided updates to the Ghostwriter engine.

constructors.description: Creates the per-module guided processing goal. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWPerModuleMojo as a mojo that processes documents for the current Maven module, runs once per module in the active reactor, and uses standard Maven reactor dependency ordering instead of aggregator-style traversal.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, install or invoke the plugin by its Maven coordinates. Step 2, choose a module-local scan root such as src/site. Step 3, optionally supply shared instruction locations and exclude patterns. Step 4, run the per-module goal when guided processing should follow standard Maven reactor dependency ordering for each module.

constructors.description: Creates the interactive action goal implementation. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActMojo as the Maven goal gw:act that runs an interactive, predefined "action" over a documentation tree. An action is documented as a prompt, typically sourced from a resource bundle or prompt file, that is applied to scanned documents. If -Dgw.act is not provided, the goal prompts the user interactively via Maven's Prompter component.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, install the plugin or invoke it directly by full coordinates. Step 2, select the documentation tree to scan with -Dgw.path. Step 3, provide a practical action prompt with -Dgw.act. Step 4, Maven instantiates org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActMojo and applies the prompt-driven transformation to scanned documents.

How to use it: Step 1, run the action goal without -Dgw.act when you want interactive prompt collection. Step 2, enter the requested action text through Maven's prompter. Step 3, let the plugin process the scanned documents using the collected action.

constructors.description: Creates the per-module action goal implementation. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActPerModuleMojo as the Maven goal gw:act-per-module that runs an action against the execution-root project using Maven's standard reactor build context and delegates scanning to ActProcessor.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, choose the execution-root directory of the Maven reactor. Step 2, provide an action prompt and an optional scan path. Step 3, run the per-module action goal when prompt-driven updates should align with standard reactor execution instead of aggregator discovery.

constructors.description: Creates the cleanup goal implementation. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.CleanMojo as the Maven goal that deletes temporary artifacts created by Machai generative-workflow document processing and notes that it is typically bound to Maven's clean phase.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, run guided or action-based Ghostwriter processing. Step 2, invoke the clean goal from the current project or module root. Step 3, let Maven create org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.CleanMojo and remove temporary workflow artifacts under the base directory.

constructors.description: Creates an empty helper instance for function-tool integrations. Javadoc states that projects must be registered later through scanProjectClasses(org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject) before tool invocations can resolve class information.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the helper when plugin runtime code needs function-tool support for project-aware class lookup. Step 2, register one or more Maven projects before invoking lookup functions. Step 3, use the helper during AI-assisted workflows that need controlled classpath inspection.

constructors.description: Creates a helper instance backed by the supplied Maven project's classpath. Javadoc documents this constructor as using the provided Maven project to resolve classpath and output directories for later class discovery and reflective inspection.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, obtain the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject from plugin execution context. Step 2, construct org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools with that project to make its compile and output classpaths available. Step 3, call the documented lookup operations during workflow execution.

constructors.description: Creates a holder for a single Maven project and eagerly initializes class discovery metadata. Javadoc states that the class builds a dedicated java.net.URLClassLoader from the project's compile classpath together with its main and test output directories, then records physical locations and artifact coordinates for public and protected classes visible from that classpath.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the holder with the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject. Step 2, allow it to scan compile and output paths eagerly. Step 3, reuse the holder for class lookup, source-path resolution, classpath-origin lookup, and dependency-artifact identification inside AI-assisted plugin workflows.

features.name: gw:gw
features.description: Aggregator goal for guided file processing. Javadoc documents org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo as the Maven goal gw:gw that runs Ghostwriter guided file processing for a project, can be executed even when a pom.xml is not present in the current directory, and processes files in reverse order so sub-modules are handled before parent modules.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, place embedded @guidance: blocks in the files you want Ghostwriter to maintain. Step 2, run the aggregator goal from the project root or another target directory. Step 3, review the updated files after guided processing completes.

How to use it: Step 1, enable multi-threaded module processing with -Dgw.threads=true when parallel handling is desired. Step 2, limit the scan root to src/site and turn on input logging for better execution visibility. Step 3, inspect the results and logs produced by the workflow.

features.name: gw:gw-per-module
features.description: Reactor-oriented guided processing goal. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWPerModuleMojo as processing documents for the current Maven module and running once per module in the active Maven reactor while Maven determines module execution order using the standard reactor dependency graph.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, start from the execution root of the Maven build. Step 2, restrict scanning to a module documentation directory such as src/site. Step 3, let Maven execute the goal once per module in dependency order.

How to use it: Step 1, provide shared instruction locations and exclude patterns. Step 2, resolve credentials from settings.xml through a Maven server id. Step 3, run the goal so each module is processed with consistent workflow configuration.

features.name: gw:act
features.description: Interactive action goal. Javadoc documents org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActMojo as a goal that runs an interactive, predefined "action" over a documentation tree, where the action is a prompt applied to scanned documents and can be provided directly or collected interactively through Maven's Prompter.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, choose a concrete editing task such as rewriting headings. Step 2, point the goal at the desired scan root. Step 3, run the goal and inspect the updated documentation produced from the action prompt.

How to use it: Step 1, prepare a directory containing predefined action definitions. Step 2, pass it through -Dgw.acts and enable input logging if needed. Step 3, run the goal so the plugin can combine documented action support with scanned documents.

features.name: gw:act-per-module
features.description: Per-module action goal. Javadoc describes org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActPerModuleMojo as running an action against the execution-root project using Maven's standard reactor build context and delegating scanning to ActProcessor.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, select the reactor execution root. Step 2, provide a prompt for the change you want to apply. Step 3, run the goal to execute prompt-driven updates while following standard Maven reactor processing.

features.name: gw:clean
features.description: Cleanup goal that removes temporary artifacts created during Machai generative-workflow document processing. Javadoc notes that this goal is typically bound to Maven's clean phase and deletes workflow temporary files under the current module base directory.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, complete one or more guided or action-based processing runs. Step 2, invoke the cleanup goal from the project or module root. Step 3, verify that temporary workflow files under the current base directory have been removed.

features.name: ClassFunctionalTools.findClass
features.signature: public java.lang.String findClass(java.lang.String className, java.io.File projectDir)
features.description: Finds fully qualified class names whose simple names match the supplied regular expression. Javadoc describes this as a function-tool integration for discovering Java classes relative to one or more registered Maven projects.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the helper with an active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject or register projects first. Step 2, call findClass with a regular expression matching simple class names such as .*Mojo. Step 3, use the returned fully qualified class names inside workflow logic that needs class discovery.

features.name: ClassFunctionalTools.getClassInfo
features.signature: public java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> getClassInfo(java.lang.String className, java.io.File projectDir)
features.description: Returns reflective information for a specified class. Javadoc states that the generated output may include modifiers, superclass, interfaces, non-private fields, constructors, non-private methods, annotations, class path, and dependency artifact coordinates.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools(project);
java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> info = tools.getClassInfo(
examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, construct the helper with a registered Maven project context. Step 2, request reflective information for a fully qualified class name. Step 3, forward the returned structured metadata to the surrounding AI-assisted processing logic.

features.name: ClassInfoHolder
features.description: Project-scoped class discovery holder. Javadoc documents that it builds a dedicated java.net.URLClassLoader from the project's compile classpath together with its main and test output directories, scans visible classes, and records physical locations and artifact coordinates for public and protected classes that can be loaded from that classpath.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, initialize the holder with the current org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject. Step 2, let it build class discovery metadata eagerly. Step 3, reuse the holder for findClasses, getClassPath, getSourcePath, getArtifactId, and loadClass operations during workflow execution.

customizations.name: gw.model
customizations.description: Provider or model identifier forwarded to the workflow. Javadoc documents this inherited parameter for the plugin goals and the effective build defines gw.model with the value CodeMie:gpt-5.5-2026-04-24.

examples.description: Select the provider and model identifier to be used by the Ghostwriter processing workflow during guided execution.

customizations.name: gw.path
customizations.description: Optional scan root override. Javadoc describes this as the configurable scan root used to determine where files containing @guidance: blocks are searched. When omitted, the default depends on the goal context such as execution root directory or current module base directory.

examples.description: Restrict workflow scanning to a module documentation directory instead of scanning a broader project tree.

customizations.name: gw.instructions
customizations.description: Instruction locations consumed by the workflow. Javadoc describes this parameter as supporting file paths or classpath locations so additional instruction sources can complement embedded guidance blocks.

examples.description: Add a shared instruction source that supplements embedded @guidance: blocks during document processing.

customizations.name: gw.excludes
customizations.description: Exclude patterns or paths skipped during scanning. Javadoc documents this as a way to avoid processing generated folders, dependencies, or other irrelevant paths while scanning documentation sources.

examples.description: Exclude generated or unrelated directories from the scan so processing stays focused on intended content.

customizations.name: genai.serverId
customizations.description: Maven settings.xml server identifier used to resolve GenAI credentials. Package and overview Javadoc document that when this property is set, the plugin reads the matching <server> entry from ~/.m2/settings.xml and forwards credentials and additional configuration fields to the workflow. The effective build defines the default value CodeMie.

examples.description: Reuse credentials stored in standard Maven settings.xml so the workflow can authenticate to the selected GenAI backend without embedding secrets in project files.

customizations.name: logInputs
customizations.description: Boolean flag controlling whether the list of workflow input files is logged. Javadoc documents this inherited parameter for the common plugin goals and indicates a default of false.

examples.description: Enable logging of scanned input files when troubleshooting or auditing action-based processing runs.

customizations.name: gw.threads
customizations.description: Goal-specific parameter defined by org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo. Javadoc documents it as enabling or disabling multi-threaded module processing for the aggregator guided processing goal, with a default of false.

examples.description: Enable multi-threaded module processing when using the aggregator guided goal and parallel execution is desired.

customizations.name: gw.act
customizations.description: Action text or prompt to apply. Javadoc documents this parameter for org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.ActMojo and states that if it is omitted, the goal reads the value interactively using Maven's Prompter component.

examples.description: Provide a direct prompt from the command line for one-off action-driven documentation changes.

customizations.name: gw.acts
customizations.description: Optional directory containing predefined action definitions. Javadoc documents this parameter for action goals, and the effective build also defines a gw.acts property pointing to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/machanism-org/machai/refs/heads/main/acts as a reusable acts catalog location.

examples.description: Point the action goal at a directory containing predefined action definitions that can be applied to scanned documents.
Use a remote acts catalog location when action-oriented workflows should resolve reusable predefined actions from a shared source.

examples.title: Install, configure, and run guided documentation processing
examples.scenario: Use the artifact as a ready-to-use Maven plugin for guided Ghostwriter updates over files containing embedded @guidance: blocks.
steps: Step 1: Add the plugin declaration for org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to the build/plugins section of pom.xml.
Step 2: Prepare maintained files, commonly under src/site, and add embedded @guidance: blocks where guided processing should occur.
Step 3: If your selected model backend requires credentials, add a matching <server> entry to ~/.m2/settings.xml and reference it with -Dgenai.serverId=CodeMie.
Step 4: Choose the model with -Dgw.model and the scan root with -Dgw.path.
Step 5: Run the guided processing goal from the project root and review the updated files after execution.
examples.description: This practical how-to scenario shows how to install the Maven plugin, configure provider credentials through Maven settings.xml, define the scan root and model, and run the guided Ghostwriter workflow from a standard Maven command.

examples.title: Run a predefined or interactive action over documentation files
examples.scenario: Use the action goal to apply a prompt-driven transformation to scanned documentation content.
steps: Step 1: Ensure org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT is declared in pom.xml or invoke it by full coordinates.
Step 2: Decide which directory to scan, for example src/site.
Step 3: Provide a prompt with -Dgw.act for predefined execution, or omit it to let the plugin collect the prompt interactively.
Step 4: Optionally point -Dgw.acts to a directory of predefined action definitions.
Step 5: Run the goal and inspect the resulting updates before committing them.
examples.description: This example demonstrates the practical ways to use the ready-to-run action goal: supplying a prompt directly, pointing to predefined action definitions, or entering the action interactively during execution.

examples.title: Process a multi-module build in per-module reactor mode
examples.scenario: Use the per-module guided processing goal when standard Maven reactor dependency ordering is preferred over aggregator traversal.
steps: Step 1: Open the execution-root directory of the Maven reactor build.
Step 2: Optionally configure credentials in ~/.m2/settings.xml if the selected model backend requires them.
Step 3: Run gw:gw-per-module with a scan root such as src/site.
Step 4: Add instruction locations and exclude patterns if needed for shared workflow control.
Step 5: Review each module's results after the reactor build completes.
examples.description: This how-to scenario shows when to choose the per-module guided goal so processing aligns with Maven reactor ordering while still using the shared Ghostwriter integration model.

examples.title: Use class discovery helpers during AI-assisted plugin workflows
examples.scenario: Create the documented tools-package helpers so plugin workflows can inspect project and dependency classes in a controlled way.
steps: Step 1: Obtain the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject from plugin execution context.
Step 2: Create org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassInfoHolder or org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools for that project.
Step 3: Use the holder or helper to discover matching classes, inspect reflective metadata, or resolve classpath origins and source paths.
Step 4: Pass the structured metadata into the surrounding Ghostwriter workflow when class-awareness improves the quality of AI-assisted processing.

code.description: This example shows how the documented helper classes can be created and then used to obtain structured reflective metadata for a project class during workflow execution.

examples.description: This practical scenario explains how the tools package supports AI-assisted workflows that need controlled introspection of project and dependency classes.

examples.title: Clean temporary Ghostwriter workflow artifacts
examples.scenario: Run the maintenance goal that removes temporary files created during guided or action-based workflows.
steps: Step 1: Execute one or more processing goals such as gw:gw or gw:act.
Step 2: Run the cleanup goal from the current project or module root.
Step 3: Verify that temporary workflow artifacts have been removed from the working tree.
examples.description: This example provides a straightforward how-to workflow for removing temporary files created by Ghostwriter processing.
